howItWorks.intro: This geothermal savings calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Net Cost = System Cost - (System Cost × Tax Credit%)
Annual Savings = Current Bill - Geothermal Bill
howItWorks.outro: Energy costs and incentives change by region and season. Check with your local utility for current rates.

faq.question: How much does geothermal save on heating and cooling?
faq.answer: Geothermal heat pumps typically reduce heating and cooling costs by 50-70%. With a COP of 4.0, you get 4 units of heating for every 1 unit of electricity — 3-4× more efficient than conventional systems.

faq.question: How much does a geothermal system cost?
faq.answer: Residential geothermal: $15,000-$35,000 installed, depending on home size and loop type. The 30% federal tax credit (through 2032) brings this down significantly. Systems last 25-50 years.

faq.question: What is COP (Coefficient of Performance)?
faq.answer: COP measures heat pump efficiency: heat output ÷ electrical input. A COP of 4.0 means 400% efficiency — 4 units of heat per 1 unit of electricity. Geothermal COP ranges from 3.5-5.0.

faq.question: Is the Geothermal Savings Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard practical calculation formulas trusted by users. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

sections.title: What Is the Geothermal Savings Calculator?
sections.body: The Geothermal Savings Calculator is a free online tool designed for users who need quick, accurate calculations in the practical calculation space. By entering your home size, current annual heating/cooling bill, geothermal system cost, you get instant results including net system cost, annual savings, payback period. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting net system cost right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In practical calculation, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by users worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to get accurate results with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your home size and need to find the right net system cost. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Home Size (sq ft)
howToSteps.description: Start by entering your home size — this is the primary input for the calculation.

howToSteps.title: Fill In Additional Details
howToSteps.description: Complete the remaining fields: current annual heating/cooling bill, geothermal system cost, federal tax credit, geothermal cop. Each value refines the calculation for greater accuracy.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your net system cost, annual savings, payback period. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your home size before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
Run the calculator with different values to compare scenarios and find the optimal approach for your situation.
Pay attention to both net system cost and annual savings — they work together to give you the full picture.
Bookmark this page for quick access next time you need to get accurate results.
If you're unsure about your geothermal cop, start with a conservative estimate and adjust from there.
